table.name: ac: car speed hysteresis
table.description: When the car is above this disable speed threshold, disable the A/C compressor. When the car is below this enable speed threshold, enable the A/C compressor.

table.name: ac: coolant hysteresis
table.description: When the engine coolant is above this disable temperature threshold, disable the A/C compressor. When the engine coolant is below this enable temperature threshold, enable the A/C compressor.

table.name: ac: engine speed hysteresis
table.description: When the engine is above this disable speed threshold, disable the A/C compressor. When the engine is below this enable speed threshold, enable the A/C compressor.

table.name: ac: pps disable time min
table.description: If the A/C compressor is disabled by the pps position threshold, it will be held off for at least this min amount of time.

table.name: ac: pps hysteresis
table.description: When the pps is above this disable position threshold, disable the A/C compressor. When the tps is below this enable position threshold, enable the A/C compressor.

table.name: ac: runtime min
table.description: The engine has to be running at least this min amount of time, before the A/C compressor can be turned on.

table.name: ac: user disable time min
table.description: Once the A/C is disabled by the user, the A/C compressor will be held off at least this min amount of time. Only afterwards it will be allowed to be turned back on.

table.name: ac: user enable time min
table.description: Once the A/C is enabled by the user, the A/C compressor will be held on at least this min amount of time. Only afterwards it will be allowed to be turned back off.

table.name: dfso: car speed hysteresis
table.description: When the car is above this enable speed threshold, enable the deceleration fuel shut off. When the car is below this disable speed threshold, disable the deceleration fuel shut off.

table.name: dfso: coolant hysteresis
table.description: When the engine coolant is above this enable temperature threshold, enable the deceleration fuel shut off. When the engine coolant is below this disable temperature threshold, disable the deceleration fuel shut off.

table.name: dfso: delay
table.description: A delay is used before activating deceleration fuel shut off.

table.name: dfso: delay recovery multiplier
table.description: In certain conditions, the DFSO delay is consumed. If this parameter is set to 1, the delay will recover in the same amount of time that has been consumed.

table.name: dfso: engine speed hysteresis
table.description: When the engine is above this enable speed threshold, enable the deceleration fuel shut off. When the engine is below this disable speed threshold, disable the deceleration fuel shut off.

table.name: dfso: pps hysteresis
table.description: When the pps is above this disable position threshold, disable the deceleration fuel shut off. When the pps is below this enable position threshold, enable the deceleration fuel shut off.

table.name: dfso: runtime min
table.description: Minimum engine run time to allow deceleration fuel shut off.

table.name: ecu: engine speed byte coefficient
table.description:
DO NOT EDIT THIS VALUE. Internal engine speed byte used within the ECU to index many lookup tables.
Formula: engine_speed = COEFFICIENT / flywheel_tooth_count_period - offset.

table.name: ecu: engine speed byte offset
table.description:
DO NOT EDIT THIS VALUE. Internal engine speed byte used within the ECU to index many lookup tables.
Formula: engine_speed = coefficient / flywheel_tooth_count_period - OFFSET.

table.name: ecu: fuelpump prime
table.description: When the ignition is switched on, the ECU energises the fuel pump during this amount of time to prime the system before switching off.

table.name: ecu: ign min
table.description: By turning the key on the IGN position, power is asserted to a dedicated input of the ECU and is considered active if above this min threshold.

table.name: ecu: shutdown delay
table.description: HEAT SOAK: How long should the ECU running after turning the key on the OFF position.

table.name: ecu: startrelay runtime max
table.description: When the engine has run longer than this max time threshold, disable the start relay.

table.name: ecu: unlock magic
table.description: Unlocked ECU have a read/write access through CAN-Bus (Live-Tuning).

table.name: evap: closedloop delay
table.description: Closed loop entry delay.

table.name: evap: dfso recovery delay
table.description: DFSO recovery delay.

table.name: evap: duty cycle initial
table.description: Initial duty cycle used during initial period.

table.name: evap: duty cycle initial time
table.description: Time to use initial duty cycle before calculated duty.

table.name: evap: duty cycle limit
table.description: Limit high allowed duty cycle for purge. Limit low allowed duty cycle for purge.

table.name: evap: engine start delay
table.description: Engine-off to engine-on delay.

table.name: evap: flow divisor
table.description: Flow divisor for scaled calculation.

table.name: evap: idle flow hysteresis
table.description: When the idle flow is above this enable threshold (and injection time is above its threshold), enable the evaporative canister purge. When the idle flow is below this disable threshold, disable the evaporative canister purge.

table.name: evap: initial delay
table.description: Initial delay before purge starts.

table.name: evap: inj time hysteresis
table.description: When the injection time is above this enable threshold (and idle flow is above its threshold), enable the evaporative canister purge. When the injection time is below this disable threshold, disable the evaporative canister purge.

table.name: evap: leak P0442 threshold
table.description: When the difference between the two pressure samples is above this pressure threshold, a small leak is detected.

table.name: evap: leak P0442 time
table.description: Leak test: small leak measurement window

table.name: evap: leak P0456 threshold
table.description: When the difference between the two pressure samples is above this pressure threshold, a very small leak is detected.

table.name: evap: leak P0456 time
table.description: Leak test: very small leak measurement window

table.name: evap: leak atmo pressure min
table.description: Leak test: minimum atmospheric pressure

table.name: evap: leak baseline time
table.description: Leak test: baseline pressure measurement duration (also divisor in state 5 compensation)

table.name: evap: leak coolant threshold
table.description: Leak test: minimum coolant temperature Leak test: maximum intake air temperature

table.name: evap: leak cooldown
table.description: Leak test: cooldown after P0442 or evap flag

table.name: evap: leak engine air stop max
table.description: Leak test: maximum air temp at engine stop

table.name: evap: leak force
table.description: Leak test: force test to run on next idle

table.name: evap: leak fuel level threshold
table.description: Leak test: minimum fuel level Leak test: maximum fuel level

table.name: evap: leak gross pressure
table.description: Leak test: large leak positive pressure threshold

table.name: evap: leak initial delay
table.description: Leak test: initial wait before closing vent valve

table.name: evap: leak purge min
table.description: Leak test: minimum purge command to pass (first occurrence)

table.name: evap: leak purge retry min
table.description: Leak test: minimum purge command to pass (after prior P0442)

table.name: evap: leak purge time
table.description: Leak test: P0455 gross leak check purge duration

table.name: evap: leak settle time
table.description: Leak test: vent valve settle time after closing

table.name: evap: leak vacuum min
table.description: Leak test: minimum vacuum magnitude (code negates before comparing evap_pressure)

table.name: evap: learn delay
table.description: Countdown duration for the evap learning delay. Reset to this value when learning conditions are not met.

table.name: evap: period
table.description: TODO: Check scale, it could be u16_time_50ns too.

table.name: evap: pressure drop dec initial
table.description: Initial pressure drop decrement.

table.name: evap: pressure drop idle limit h
table.description: Limit high pressure drop during idle.

table.name: evap: pressure drop inc limit l
table.description: Limit low/initial pressure drop increment.

table.name: evap: pressure drop max
table.description: Maximum permitted canister pressure drop for a given intake manifold vacuum.

table.name: evap: purge
table.description:
The evaporative system purge valve is normally closed. The opening duty cycle is calculated based on the intake vacuum and the pressure drop across the canister.
Formula: opening = duty_cylce_min + PURGE

table.name: evap: purge mode
table.description: Evaporative system purge mode.

table.name: evap: restart delay
table.description: Long delay for restart condition.

table.name: evap: stft stability delay
table.description: STFT stability check delay.

table.name: fan: car speed disable
table.description: When the car is above this disable speed threshold, turn off the fan.

table.name: fan: high ac hysteresis
table.description: WITH A/C ON: When the engine coolant is above this enable temperature threshold, turn on the high speed fan. WITH A/C ON: When the engine coolant is below this disable temperature threshold, turn off the high speed fan.

table.name: fan: high hysteresis
table.description: When the engine coolant is above this enable temperature threshold, turn on the high speed fan. When the engine coolant is below this disable temperature threshold, turn off the high speed fan.

table.name: fan: high stop time min
table.description: The high speed fan will be held off for at least this min amount of time. Only afterwards it will be allowed to be turned back on.

table.name: fan: high stop user delay
table.description: The signal AC REQ FAN also controls the high speed fan. But when this signal is deasserted, a delay defined by this value is used before the fan is turned off.

table.name: fan: low hysteresis
table.description: When the engine coolant is above this enable temperature threshold, turn on the low speed fan. When the engine coolant is below this disable temperature threshold, turn off the low speed fan.

table.name: fan: low stop hysteresis
table.description: WITH ENGINE OFF (HEAT SOAK): When the engine coolant is above this enable temperature threshold, turn on the low speed fan. WITH ENGINE OFF (HEAT SOAK): When the engine coolant is below this disable temperature threshold, turn off the low speed fan.

table.name: fan: low stop time min
table.description: The low speed fan will be held off for at least this min amount of time. Only afterwards it will be allowed to be turned back on.

table.name: idle: flow adj1 corr limit h
table.description: Limit high for the LEA_idle_flow_adj1 value. The ECU will not make correction above this value.

table.name: idle: flow adj1 corr limit l
table.description: Limit low for the LEA_idle_flow_adj1 value. The ECU will not make correction below this value.

table.name: idle: flow adj1 corr maf accumulated min

table.data: flow adj1 corr maf accumulated min

table.description: The ECU will not make correction to the learned_flow_adj1 value, unless the air is above this min mass threshold.

table.name: idle: flow adj1 corr max
table.description: The ECU will make a +10.24mg/s correction to the learned_flow_adj1 value, if the flow_adj8 is above this max threshold.

table.name: idle: flow adj1 corr min
table.description: The ECU will make a -10.24mg/s correction to the learned_flow_adj1 value, if the flow_adj8 is below this min threshold.

table.name: idle: flow adj2
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - ADJ2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow adj3
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - adj2 + ADJ3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow adj4
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - adj2 + adj3 + (ADJ4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow adj4 adj
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - adj2 + adj3 + (adj4 * ADJ4_ADJ) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow adj5
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - adj2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + ADJ5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow adj5 max
table.description: When the car is below this max speed threshold, the flow_adj5 (and adj1_alternative instead of adj1) will be used.

table.name: idle: flow adj6
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - adj2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + ADJ6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow adj7
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - adj2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + ADJ7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow adj7 hysteresis
table.description: Below this disable threshold (relative to target_speed), flow_adj7 is disabled. Between disable and enable thresholds, adj7 is factorized. Above this enable threshold (relative to target_speed), flow_adj7 is fully enabled. Between disable and enable thresholds, adj7 is factorized.

table.name: idle: flow adj8 corr limit h
table.description: Limit high flow_adj8 value. The ECU will not make correction above this value.

table.name: idle: flow adj8 corr limit l
table.description: Limit low flow_adj8 value. The ECU will not make correction below this value.

table.name: idle: flow adj8 corr step
table.description:
When the engine speed is not equal to the speed_target, adj8 will be corrected by this value.
Formula: flow_adj8 = flow_adj8 +/- flow_adj8_corr_step

table.name: idle: flow adj8 corr time between step

table.data: flow adj8 corr time between step

table.description: Minimum time between ECU correction of the flow_adj8 value.

table.name: idle: flow adj ac
table.description:
WITH A/C ON: Compensate the power of the A/C compressor.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - ADJ2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + ADJ_AC + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow

table.name: idle: flow adj fan high
table.description:
WITH FAN HIGH ON: Compensate the power of the high speed fan.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - ADJ2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_low_fan + ADJ_HIGH_FAN - evap_flow

table.name: idle: flow adj fan low
table.description:
WITH FAN LOW ON: Compensate the power of the low speed fan.
Formula: flow = flow_base + learned_adj1 - ADJ2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + ADJ_FAN_LOW + adj_fan_high - evap_flow

table.name: idle: flow base
table.description:
Compute the needed engine power (air flow) to keep a stabilized idling.
Formula: flow = FLOW_BASE + learned_adj1 - adj2 + adj3 + (adj4 * adj4_adj) + adj5 + adj6 + adj7 + adj8 + adj_ac + adj_fan_low + adj_fan_high - evap_flow
Note: adj8 is a dynamic adjustment.

table.name: idle: flow pps max
table.description: When the pps is above this max position threshold, disable the correction of the adj1_learned, adj8 and more...

table.name: idle: flow time between decrement
table.description: When the flow is reduced, it will be smoothly decremented (-0.1g/s) using the flow_time_between_decrement time interval.

table.name: idle: speed adj
table.description:
Desired engine idle speed.
Formula: speed_target = speed_base + SPEED_ADJ + speed_adj_ac_on

table.name: idle: speed adj ac on
table.description:
Desired engine idle speed.
Formula: speed_target = speed_base + speed_adj + SPEED_ADJ_AC_ON

table.name: idle: speed base
table.description:
Desired engine idle speed.
Formula: speed_target = SPEED_BASE + speed_adj + speed_adj_ac_on

table.name: idle: speed time between decrement
table.description: Desired engine idle speed. When the speed is reduced, it will be smoothly decremented (-10rpm) using the speed_time_between_decrement time interval.

table.name: idle: tps
table.description: Once the needed air flow to keep the engine idling is knowned, the ECU must drive the throttle body to the according position. The differential air pressure over the throttle body is estimated.

table.name: idle: tps engine stop
table.description: Throttle body position for cranking.

table.name: idle: tps limit h
table.description: Limit high allowed tps position for idling. Note: tps_max does not limit the tps engine stop value.

table.name: ign: adv adj1
table.description:
Ignition advance adjustement with the engine air temperature.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + ADJ1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + ADJ1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv adj2
table.description:
Ignition advance adjustement for startup.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * ADJ2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * ADJ2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv adj3
table.description:
Ignition advance adjustement with the engine speed.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (ADJ3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (ADJ3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv adj3 adj
table.description:
Ignition advance adjustement with the engine speed.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * ADJ3_ADJ) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * ADJ3_ADJ) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv adj4
table.description:
Ignition advance adjustement with the change in the tps.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - ADJ4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - ADJ4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv adj cyl
table.description: Ignition advance adjustment per cylinder.

table.name: ign: adv cranking
table.description:
Ignition advance (degree before top dead center) when cranking.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = ADV_CRANKING

table.name: ign: adv dfso
table.description:
Ignition advance (degree before top dead center) when dfso is activated.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso): adv = ADV_DFSO Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv high cam base
table.description:
Ignition advance (degree before top dead center) when the VVL is engaged and engine speed is below vvl_rpm_enable (by default 6200 RPM).
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = HIGH_CAM_BASE * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv high cam knock safe
table.description: Knock safe ignition advance used by knock_retard2.

table.name: ign: adv idle adj1
table.description:
Ignition advance idle adjustment.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + IDLE_ADJ1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv idle adj1 alternative
table.description:
Ignition advance idle adjustment.
Note: The ignition adj1_alternative will be used instead of adj1, when the car is below the idle_flow_adj5_threshold speed.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + ADJ1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + ADJ1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv idle adj1 time
table.description: Duration over which ign_adv_idle_adj1 fades back to zero after idle transition condition clears

table.name: ign: adv idle adj2
table.description:
Ignition advance idle adjustment.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + IDLE_ADJ2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv idle adj3
table.description:
Ignition advance idle adjustment.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + IDLE_ADJ3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv idle adj3 engine speed dt age

table.data: adv idle adj3 engine speed dt age

table.description: Engine speed differential, the current speed and an old speed are used. This value sets how old should be the second value. Between 0 and 10 (10 is the less sensitive).

table.name: ign: adv idle adj ac on
table.description:
Ignition advance adjustment when A/C is on.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + IDLE_ADJ_AC_ON Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv idle base
table.description:
Ignition advance (degree before top dead center) when the engine is idling.
Formula (low cam) : adv = low_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = IDLE_BASE + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv limit l
table.description: Limit low allowable ignition advance.

table.name: ign: adv low cam base
table.description:
Ignition advance (degree before top dead center) when the VVL is disengaged or when engine speed is above vvl_rpm_enable (by default 6200 RPM).
Formula (low cam) : adv = LOW_CAM_BASE * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (high cam): adv = high_cam_base * adj2 * tc_adj + adj1 + (adj3 * adj3_adj) - adj4 Formula (idle) : adv = idle_base + idle_adj1 + idle_adj2 + idle_adj3 + idle_adj_ac_on Formula (dfso) : adv = adv_dfso Formula (cranking): adv = adv_cranking

table.name: ign: adv low cam knock safe
table.description: Knock safe ignition advance used by knock_retard2.

table.name: ign: adv smooth step
table.description: The ignition advance timing is adjusted smoothly in steps defined by this table every 50 ms.

table.name: ign: cranking runtime max
table.description: Maximum time after startup to use the cranking ignition advance.

table.name: ign: dwell base
table.description: Ignition dwell time.

table.name: ign: stop coolant to use adj2 threshold
table.description: When the engine coolant is below this min startup temperature threshold, adj2 is always 0. When the engine coolant is above this max startup temperature threshold, adj2 is always 0.

table.name: inj: afr adj per adv adj
table.description:
Fuel enrichment based on ignition advance (No enleanment allowed).
Formula:
afr_adj = AFR_ADJ_PER_ADV_ADJ * (knock_ign_retard2 - ign_adv_adj1 - ign_adv_adj3)
a = (load / (afr_base - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (efficiency * flow_rate) c = b * adj1 * adj2 * adj3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = base + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: inj: afr base
table.description:
Ratio between air and fuel.
Formula:
a = (load / (AFR_BASE - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (efficiency * flow_rate) c = b * adj1 * adj2 * adj3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = base + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: inj: angle
table.description: Target engine position to begin fuel injection (with an offset for each cylinder's relative position).

table.name: inj: efficiency
table.description:
Injector efficiency. Depending on the fuel rail and the manifold pressure, the efficiency of the injectors change.
Formula:
a = (load / (afr_base - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (EFFICIENCY * flow_rate) c = b * adj1 * adj2 * adj3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = base + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: inj: flow rate
table.description:
Injector size: 4600mg/s = 330cc/min and 6200mg/s = 440cc/min. This parameter doesn't affect the tip-in/out value, change the base time of tip accordingly.
Formula:
a = (load / (afr_base - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (efficiency * FLOW_RATE) c = b * adj1 * adj2 * adj3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = base + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: inj: time adj1
table.description:
Fueling adjustment at startup. It's like a carburetor choke. The last line should always be 100%.
Formula:
a = (load / (afr_base - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (efficiency * flow_rate) c = b * ADJ1 * adj2 * adj3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = base + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: inj: time adj2
table.description:
Fueling adjustment with the air temperature.
Formula:
a = (load / (afr_base - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (efficiency * flow_rate) c = b * adj1 * ADJ2 * adj3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = base + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: inj: time adj3
table.description:
Fueling adjustment with the coolant temperature.
Formula:
a = (load / (afr_base - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (efficiency * flow_rate) c = b * adj1 * adj2 * ADJ3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = base + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: inj: time adj cranking
table.description:
Fueling adjustment when cranking.
Formula: inj_time = base + adj_cranking

table.name: inj: time base
table.description:
Injector dead time.
Formula:
a = (load / (afr_base - afr_adj)) - evap b = a / (efficiency * flow_rate) c = b * adj1 * adj2 * adj3 + tip_in + tip_out + reactive d = BASE + ltft_idle + (1 + stft + ltft) * c
inj_time = d

table.name: injtip: catalyst adj
table.description:
During DFSO the catalyst is oxidized. Add extra fuel (injection time) to reactivate the catalyst.
Formula: reactivate = (time_base * ADJ) * (adj_fadeout ^ t)

table.name: injtip: catalyst adj fadeout
table.description:
During DFSO the catalyst is oxidized. Add extra fuel (injection time) to reactivate the catalyst.
Formula: reactivate = (time_base * adj) * (ADJ_FADEOUT ^ t)

table.name: injtip: in adj1
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on positive change in the tps. It's like the accelerator pump of a carburator.
Formula: tip_in(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (adj2 * t)) * ADJ1 * adj3 * adj_gear

table.name: injtip: in adj2
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on positive change in the tps. It's like the accelerator pump of a carburator.
This adjustment will reduce the effect over time (t).
Formula: tip_in(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (ADJ2 * t)) * adj1 * adj3 * adj_gear

table.name: injtip: in adj3
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on positive change in the tps. It's like the accelerator pump of a carburator.
Formula: tip_in(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (adj2 * t)) * adj1 * ADJ3 * adj_gear

table.name: injtip: in adj gears
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on change in the tps. It's like the accelerator pump of a carburator.
Formula: tip_in(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (adj2 * t)) * adj1 * adj3 * ADJ_GEAR

table.name: injtip: in adj gears 6
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on change in the tps. It's like the accelerator pump of a carburator.
Formula: tip_in(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (adj2 * t)) * adj1 * adj3 * ADJ_GEAR

table.name: injtip: in dt tps target 2 limit
table.description:
Positive limit value for dt_tps_target_2.
Formula: tip_out(t) = time_base * (DT_TPS_TARGET_2 - (adj2 * t)) * adj1 * adj3

table.name: injtip: in dt tps target 2 min
table.description: Minimum dt_tps_target_2 value to trigger the tip-in algorithm (t = 0).

table.name: injtip: out adj1
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on negative change in the tps.
Formula: tip_out(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (adj2 * t)) * ADJ1 * adj3

table.name: injtip: out adj2
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on negative change in the tps.
This adjustment will reduce the effect over time (t).
Formula: tip_out(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (ADJ2 * t)) * adj1 * adj3

table.name: injtip: out adj3
table.description:
Add extra fuel (injection time) based on negative change in the tps.
This adjustment will reduce the effect over time (t).
Formula: tip_out(t) = time_base * (dt_tps_target_2 - (adj2 * t)) * adj1 * ADJ3

table.name: injtip: out dt tps target 2 limit
table.description:
Negative limit value for dt_tps_target_2.
Formula: tip_out(t) = time_base * (DT_TPS_TARGET_2 - (adj2 * t)) * adj1 * adj3

table.name: injtip: out dt tps target 2 min
table.description: Minimum dt_tps_target_2 value to trigger the tip-out algorithm (t = 0).

table.name: injtip: time base
table.description: Standard: 60us for 330cc/min and 40us for 440cc/min injector.

table.name: knock: load margin
table.description: Engine load margin for retard_load_min and retard2_corr_load_min thresholds.

table.name: knock: peak threshold
table.description:
When the peak is above this threshold, the ECU takes it as a real knock.
Formula:
signal_avg = (100% - reactivity) * signal_avg + reactivity * signal peak = (signal * 10) / (signal_avg + 1) peak_over_threshold = peak - THRESHOLD

table.name: knock: retard1 coolant min
table.description: If the engine coolant is below this min temperature threshold, disable retard1.

table.name: knock: retard1 dt tps target 2 max
table.description: If the tps is above this max change threshold, disable retard1.

table.name: knock: retard1 dt tps target 2 time
table.description: If the retard1 is disabled by a change in tps, it will be re-enable after this amount of time.

table.name: knock: retard1 engine speed threshold
table.description: If the engine is above this max speed threshold, disable retard1. It will be re-enabled when the engine speed is below this threshold minus the speed_margin. If the engine is below this min speed threshold, disable retard1. It will be re-enabled when the engine speed is above this threshold plus the speed_margin.

table.name: knock: retard1 inc
table.description:
Increment retard1 in proportion to the knock peak.
Formula (per cylinder): retard1 = retard1 + STEP

table.name: knock: retard1 limit
table.description: Maximium allowed value for retard1.

table.name: knock: retard1 vvt diff max
table.description: Maximum VVT position ERROR allowed for knock retard to be active. Knock control is disabled when abs(vvt_target - vvt_pos) exceeds this threshold.

table.name: knock: retard2 corr dec step
table.description:
The ECU will make corrections to the retard2 value, if the engine load is above the retard2_corr_load_min.
Formula (retard1 is 0): retard2 = retard2 - RETARD2_CORR_DEC_STEP Formula (retard1 > 0): retard2 = retard2 + retard1 * retard2_corr_inc_coef
Note: retard2 is saved in the eeprom.

table.name: knock: retard2 corr inc coef
table.description:
The ECU will make corrections to the retard2 value, if the engine load is above the retard2_corr_load_min.
Formula (retard1 is 0): retard2 = retard2 - retard2_corr_dec_step Formula (retard1 > 0): retard2 = retard2 + retard1 * RETARD2_CORR_INC_COEF
Note: retard2 is saved in the eeprom.

table.name: knock: retard2 corr load min
table.description: This min load threshold enables retard2 corrections when exceeded; below threshold minus load_margin, no more corrections will occur.

table.name: knock: retard load min
table.description: This min load threshold enables knock retard1 and retard2 when exceeded; below threshold minus load_margin, retard1 resets to 0 and retard2 is no longer applied.

table.name: knock: sensitivity cylinder1
table.description: Knock sensitivity cylinder 1 value send to the L9119D.

table.name: knock: sensitivity cylinder2
table.description: Knock sensitivity cylinder 2 value send to the L9119D.

table.name: knock: sensitivity cylinder3
table.description: Knock sensitivity cylinder 3 value send to the L9119D.

table.name: knock: sensitivity cylinder4
table.description: Knock sensitivity cylinder 4 value send to the L9119D.

table.name: knock: signal fadeout step
table.description:
WHEN KNOCK IS DETECTED: Fadeout the peak by incrementing the average.
Formula: signal_avg = signal_avg + SIGNAL_FADEOUT_STEP

table.name: knock: signal reactivity
table.description:
To separate real knock from noise, a peak detection is build for every cylinders.
Formula:
signal_avg = (100% - REACTIVITY) * signal_avg + REACTIVITY * signal peak = (signal * 10) / (signal_avg + 1) peak_over_threshold = peak - threshold

table.name: knock: signal smooth limit l
table.description: Limit low value allowed for signal_smooth.

table.name: knock: speed margin
table.description: Engine speed margin for retard1_engine_speed_min and retard1_engine_speed_max thresholds.

table.name: knock: window start
table.description: Knock detection window start position relative to cylinder TDC reference tooth.

table.name: knock: window width
table.description: Knock detection window duration.

table.name: load: alphaN adj corr coolant min
table.description: The ECU will not make correction to the alphaN adj table, unless the engine coolant is above this min temperature threshold.

table.name: load: alphaN adj corr dt engine speed max

table.data: alphaN adj corr dt engine speed max

table.description: The ECU will not make correction to the alphaN adj table, if the negative or positive engine speed change is above this max limit threshold.

table.name: load: alphaN adj corr dt tps target 1 max

table.data: alphaN adj corr dt tps target 1 max

table.description: The ECU will not make correction to the alphaN adj table, if the negative or positive tps change is above this max limit threshold.

table.name: load: alphaN adj corr limit
table.description: Limit high for the LEA_load_alphaN_adj value. The ECU will not make correction above this value. Limit low for the LEA_load_alphaN_adj value. The ECU will not make correction below this value.

table.name: load: alphaN adj corr threshold
table.description: The ECU will make a -1% correction to the alphaN adj table, if the ratio between the alphaN and the maf load is above this max threshold. The ECU will make a +1% correction to the alphaN adj table, if the ratio between the alphaN and the maf load is below this min threshold.

table.name: load: alphaN adj corr time between step

table.data: alphaN adj corr time between step

table.description: Minimum time between ECU correction of the alphaN adj table.

table.name: load: alphaN adj corr time min
table.description: Once the ECU is allowed to make corrections to the alphaN adj table, wait this min amount of time before the first correction.

table.name: load: alphaN base
table.description:
Rapid changes in tps create too much turbulence to have a reliable maf information. So the ECU falls back into a traditional alphaN load calculation for a small amount of time.
An absolute tps position threshold also trigger the usage of the alphaN table.
Formula: load = BASE * (atmo_pressure / 1013mbar) * (298°K / temp_engine_air) * learned_adj
Note: temp_engine_air in Kelvin degrees.

table.name: load: alphaN engine stop
table.description:
Initial alphaN load calculation for cranking.
Formula: load = BASE * (atmo_pressure / 1013mbar) * (298°K / temp_engine_air)
Note: temp_engine_air in Kelvin degrees.

table.name: load: possible max
table.description:
The engine load is also available as a ratio to the possible max load (OBD Mode 0x01 PID 0x04).
Formula: load(%) = load(mg/stroke) / LOAD_POSSIBLE_MAX * (1013mbar / atmo_pressure)

table.name: load: possible max absolute
table.description:
The engine load is also available as a ratio to the possible max load (OBD Mode 0x01 PID 0x43).
Formula: load(%) = load(mg/stroke) / LOAD_POSSIBLE_MAX_ABSOLUTE

table.name: load: possible max mode22
table.description:
The engine load is also available as a ratio to the possible max load (OBD Mode 0x22 PID 0x022A).
Formula: load(%) = load(mg/stroke) / LOAD_MAX

table.name: load: reactivity
table.description:
Every 5ms, the sampled load value (maf or alphaN) is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: load: use alphaN dt tps target 1 negative min

table.data: use alphaN dt tps target 1 negative min

table.description: When the negative tps change (dt_tps_target_1) is above this min threshold, use the alphaN load calculation.

table.name: load: use alphaN dt tps target 1 positive min

table.data: use alphaN dt tps target 1 positive min

table.description: When the positive tps change (dt_tps_target_1) is above this min threshold, use the alphaN load calculation.

table.name: load: use alphaN engine speed max
table.description: When the engine is above this max speed limit, ignore the alphaN table (only maf).

table.name: load: use alphaN timer
table.description: Once the negative/positive tps change or the absolute tps threshold is triggered, the alphaN will be used for this amount of time.

table.name: load: use alphaN tps min
table.description: When the tps is above this min position threshold (per RPM bin), use the alphaN load calculation.

table.name: ltft: atmo min
table.description: Minimum atmospheric pressure to enable the ltft correction.

table.name: ltft: coolant min
table.description: Minimum engine coolant temperature to enable the ltft correction.

table.name: ltft: engine air min
table.description: Minimum engine air temperature to enable the ltft correction.

table.name: ltft: fuel min
table.description: Minimum fuel level to enable the ltft correction.

table.name: ltft: stft smooth threshold
table.description: When the average stft is above this max correction threshold, the ltft is incremented. When the average stft is below this min correction threshold, the ltft is decremented.

table.name: ltft: time between step
table.description: Minimum time between ltft corrections.

table.name: ltft: zone1 engine speed max
table.description: This max threshold defines the maximum engine speed for the zone1 range.

table.name: ltft: zone1 flow max
table.description: This max threshold defines the maximum maf flow for the zone1 range.

table.name: ltft: zone1 limit h
table.description: Limit high allowed value for LEA_ltft_zone1_adj.

table.name: ltft: zone1 limit l
table.description: Limit low allowed value for LEA_ltft_zone1_adj.

table.name: ltft: zone1 step
table.description:
Decrement/Increment for the ltft correction for zone1.
Formula (zone1): zone1_adj = zone1_adj -/+ ZONE1_STEP

table.name: ltft: zone2 flow threshold
table.description: This max threshold defines the maximum maf flow for the zone2 range. This min threshold defines the minimum maf flow for the zone2 range.

table.name: ltft: zone2 limit
table.description: Limit high allowed value for LEA_ltft_zone2_adj. Limit low allowed value for LEA_ltft_zone2_adj.

table.name: ltft: zone2 load threshold
table.description: This max threshold defines the maximum engine load for the zone2 range. This min threshold defines the minimum engine load for the zone2 range.

table.name: ltft: zone3 flow min
table.description: This min threshold defines the minimum maf flow for the zone3 range.

table.name: ltft: zone3 limit
table.description: Limit high allowed value for LEA_ltft_zone3_adj. Limit low allowed value for LEA_ltft_zone3_adj.

table.name: ltft: zone3 load min
table.description: This min threshold defines the minimum engine load for the zone3 range.

table.name: misc: airbox flap hysteresis
table.description: When the engine is below this disable speed threshold, close the flap in the airbox. When the engine is above this enable speed threshold, open the flap in the airbox.

table.name: misc: coolant warning max
table.description: When the engine coolant is above this max temperature threshold, display the warning on the instrument cluster.

table.name: misc: engine running crank tooth min
table.description: Minimum crank tooth count to consider the engine as started.

table.name: misc: engine running hysteresis
table.description: When the engine is above this enable speed threshold, the engine is considered as started. When the engine is below this disable speed threshold, the engine is considered as stopped.

table.name: misc: gears
table.description: The ECU estimates the current gear based on rpm and vehicle speed and these are the thresholds for that determination.

table.name: misc: gears 6
table.description: Thresholds for the 6th gear.

table.name: misc: pps 1 range range
table.description: Low end of the expected voltage range of the pedal position sensor 1. High end of the expected voltage range of the pedal position sensor 1.

table.name: misc: pps 2 range range
table.description: Low end of the expected voltage range of the pedal position sensor 2. High end of the expected voltage range of the pedal position sensor 2.

table.name: misc: recirculation pump stop hysteresis
table.description: WITH ENGINE OFF (HEAT SOAK): When the engine coolant is above this enable temperature threshold, turn on the recirculation pump. WITH ENGINE OFF (HEAT SOAK): When the engine coolant is below this disable temperature threshold, turn off the recirculation pump.

table.name: misc: tps 1 range range
table.description: Low end of the expected voltage range of the throttle position sensor 1. High end of the expected voltage range of the throttle position sensor 1.

table.name: misc: tps 2 range range
table.description: Low end of the expected voltage range of the throttle position sensor 2. High end of the expected voltage range of the throttle position sensor 2.

table.name: misc: use tmap

state.name: Use temperature of MAP sensor (after intercooler)

table.description: On supercharged car, there is an additional sensor build after the intercooler.

Level 4 DTC: Permanent, freeze frame (overwrite level 1,3)

table.description: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Pre Catalyst)

table.name: obd: P0133

state.name: Level 1 DTC: Permanent, freeze frame (no overwrite)
Level 2 DTC: Pending and Permanent, freeze frame (overwrite level 1,3)

table.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response (Pre Catalyst)

table.name: obd: P0133 consecutive
table.description: Number of consecutive occurrences before raising the trouble code.

table.name: obd: P0133 threshold1 lean2rich
table.description: P0133 Threshold 1: Throws an error code when both the LEAN-TO-RICH and rich-to-lean transitions exceed their respective time thresholds.

table.name: obd: P0133 threshold1 rich2lean
table.description: P0133 Threshold 1: Throws an error code when both the lean-to-rich and RICH-TO-LEAN transitions exceed their respective time thresholds.

table.name: obd: P0133 threshold2 lean2rich
table.description: P0133 Threshold 2: With a ratio threshold of 200% and a TIME threshold of 135ms, this means that if the lean-to-rich transition is twice as slow as the rich-to-lean transition and takes more than 135ms, it will trigger an error code.

table.name: obd: P0133 threshold2 ratio
table.description: P0133 Threshold 2: With a RATIO threshold of 200% and a time threshold of 135ms, this means that if the lean-to-rich transition is twice as slow as the rich-to-lean transition and takes more than 135ms, it will trigger an error code.

table.name: obd: P0133 threshold3 ratio
table.description: P0133 Threshold 3: With a RATIO threshold of 50% and a time threshold of 70ms, this means that if the rich-to-lean transition is twice as slow as the lean-to-rich transition and takes more than 70ms, it will trigger an error code.

table.name: obd: P0133 threshold3 rich2lean
table.description: P0133 Threshold 3: With a ratio threshold of 50% and a TIME threshold of 70ms, this means that if the rich-to-lean transition is twice as slow as the lean-to-rich transition and takes more than 70ms, it will trigger an error code.

table.name: obd: misfire threshold
table.description: Crankshaft acceleration deviation THRESHOLD for misfire detection. Lower values increase sensitivity.

state.name: Components, Fuel System, Misfire, O2 Sensor Heater, O2 Sensor, Evaporative System, Catalyst
Components, Fuel System, Misfire, O2 Sensor Heater, O2 Sensor, Catalyst
Components, Fuel System, Misfire, O2 Sensor, Catalyst

table.name: obd: warm up cycles clear freeze frame

table.data: warm up cycles clear freeze frame

table.description: Number of warm-up cycles before erasing the freeze frame.

table.name: sensor: baro gain
table.description: Barometric pressure sensor build into the ECU. Datasheet MXP4100A: 1/0.01059 * 10 mbar/kPa = 944

table.name: sensor: baro offset
table.description: Barometric pressure sensor build into the ECU. Datasheet MXP4100A: 0.1518/0.01059 * 10 mbar/kPa = 143

table.name: sensor: coolant fallback
table.description: Coolant temperature substituted when the coolant sensor is confirmed faulted.

table.name: sensor: coolant reactivity
table.description:
At every cycle, the sampled coolant value is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: sensor: coolant scaling
table.description: Scaling of the engine coolant temperature sensor.

table.name: sensor: dt engine speed age
table.description: Engine speed differential, the current speed and an old speed are used. This value sets how old should be the second value. Between 0 and 7 (7 is the less sensitive).

table.name: sensor: dt tps target 1 age
table.description: Throttle body target position differential value 1, the current tps and an old tps values are used. This value sets how old should be the second value. Between 0 and 75ms.

table.name: sensor: dt tps target 2 age
table.description: Throttle body target position differential value 2, the current tps and an old tps values are used. This value sets how old should be the second value. Between 0 and 75ms.

table.name: sensor: engine air fallback
table.description: Engine air temperature substituted when the engine air sensor is confirmed faulted.

table.name: sensor: engine air reactivity
table.description:
At every cycle, the sampled engine air value is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: sensor: engine air scaling
table.description:
Scaling of the engine air temperature sensor.
If use tmap = 0: The air temperature sensor from maf is used. If use tmap = 1: The air temperature sensor from map is used.

table.name: sensor: engine speed period avg size
table.description: Sometimes an average is used on the sampled engine speed period values. This value sets how many samples are used to build the average. Between 1 and 7 (1 no average, 7 very smooth).

table.name: sensor: evap gain
table.description:
Pressure sensor calibration of the evaporative system.
Formula: pressure = signal * GAIN + offset

table.name: sensor: evap offset
table.description:
Pressure sensor calibration of the evaporative system.
Formula: pressure = signal * gain + OFFSET

table.name: sensor: evap reactivity
table.description:
At every cycle, the sampled evaporative value is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: sensor: fuel reactivity
table.description:
At every cycle, the sampled fuel level value is mixed with the previous value. Note: This does not have an influence on the fuel gauge of the instrument cluster.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: sensor: fuel scaling
table.description: Scaling of the fuel level sensor.

table.name: sensor: intake air reactivity
table.description:
At every cycle, the sampled intake air value is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: sensor: intake air scaling
table.description: Scaling of the air temperature sensor. Build into the maf.

table.name: sensor: maf avg size
table.description: An average is used on the sampled maf values. This value sets how many samples are used to build the average. Between 1 and 31 (1 no average, 31 very smooth).

table.name: sensor: maf scaling
table.description: Scaling of the mass air flow sensor.

table.name: sensor: map avg size
table.description: An average is used on the sampled MAP values. This value sets how many samples are used to build the average. Between 1 and 31 (1 no average, 31 very smooth).

table.name: sensor: map gain
table.description:
Pressure sensor calibration of the intake manifold.
Formula: pressure = signal * GAIN + offset

table.name: sensor: map offset
table.description:
Pressure sensor calibration of the intake manifold.
Formula: pressure = signal * gain + OFFSET

table.name: sensor: o2 heater max
table.description: The ECU will stop the heater if the current exceeds this max threshold (an old or cold O2 sensor may have very low resistance).

table.name: sensor: o2 heater warmup period
table.description: The duty cylce is hard coded to 80% but the period can be changed.

table.name: sensor: o2 heater warmup time
table.description: Once the engine is running and during this WARMUP time, the O2 heater will be progressively heated up (80% heater duty cycle).

table.name: sensor: o2 scaling
table.description: Scaling of the both oxygen (lambda) sensors.

table.name: sensor: pps 1 gain
table.description:
Pedal position sensor 1 calibration.
Formula: pps1 = GAIN * (signal - offset)

table.name: sensor: pps 1 offset
table.description:
Pedal position sensor 1 calibration.
Formula: pps1 = gain * (signal - OFFSET)

table.name: sensor: pps 1 offset limit l
table.description: Lower clamp on the PPS1 learned zero offset. The learned offset will never be pushed below this value.

table.name: sensor: pps 2 gain
table.description:
Pedal position sensor 2 calibration. There is 2 potentiometers in the gas pedal for redundancy. The second is usually half of the first.
Formula: pps2 = GAIN * (signal - offset)

table.name: sensor: pps 2 offset
table.description:
Pedal position sensor 2 calibration. There is 2 potentiometers in the gas pedal for redundancy.
Formula: pps2 = gain * (signal - OFFSET)

table.name: sensor: pps 2 offset limit l
table.description: Lower clamp on the PPS2 learned zero offset. The learned offset will never be pushed below this value.

table.name: sensor: pps match max
table.description: Max percent threshold defining the allowed tolerance between pps_1 and pps_2.

table.name: sensor: pps offset diff max
table.description: Maximum deviation from the calibration default offset before the learned offset is reset. Also added to stored offsets at startup to give headroom for downward tracking.

table.name: sensor: pps offset reactivity
table.description: Learning rate for PPS zero offset tracking. Fractional step toward the minimum observed ADC reading applied each learning cycle.

table.name: sensor: pps offset time between step
table.description: Time between each PPS zero offset learning step.

table.name: sensor: pps reactivity
table.description:
Every 5ms, the sampled pps value is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: sensor: tps match max
table.description: Max percent threshold defining the allowed tolerance between tps_1 and tps_2.

table.name: stft: afr
table.description: Target afr value to enable the closed loop operation.

table.name: stft: engine air min
table.description: Minimum engine air temperature to enable the closed loop operation.

table.name: stft: enleanment initial
table.description:
When a rich condition is detected, the stft will enlean the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft - INITIAL Formula (subsequent): stft = stft - step Formula (with adj) : stft = stft - step * adj

table.name: stft: enleanment step
table.description:
When a rich condition is detected, the stft will enlean the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft - initial Formula (subsequent): stft = stft - STEP Formula (with adj) : stft = stft - STEP * adj

table.name: stft: enleanment step adj
table.description:
When a rich condition is detected, the stft will enlean the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft - initial Formula (subsequent): stft = stft - step Formula (with adj) : stft = stft - step * ADJ

table.name: stft: enrichment initial
table.description:
When a lean condition is detected, the stft will enrich the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft + INITIAL Formula (subsequent): stft = stft + step Formula (with adj) : stft = stft + step * adj

table.name: stft: enrichment step
table.description:
When a lean condition is detected, the stft will enrich the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft + initial Formula (subsequent): stft = stft + STEP Formula (with adj) : stft = stft + STEP * adj

table.name: stft: enrichment step adj
table.description:
When a lean condition is detected, the stft will enrich the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft + initial Formula (subsequent): stft = stft + step Formula (with adj) : stft = stft + step * ADJ

table.name: stft: idle enleanment initial
table.description:
WITH IDLING ENGINE: When a rich condition is detected, the stft will enlean the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft - INITIAL Formula (subsequent): stft = stft - step

table.name: stft: idle enleanment step
table.description:
WITH IDLING ENGINE: When a rich condition is detected, the stft will enlean the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft - initial Formula (subsequent): stft = stft - STEP

table.name: stft: idle enrichment initial
table.description:
WITH IDLING ENGINE: When a lean condition is detected, the stft will enrich the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft + INITIAL Formula (subsequent): stft = stft + step

table.name: stft: idle enrichment step
table.description:
WITH IDLING ENGINE: When a lean condition is detected, the stft will enrich the mixture.
Formula (first) : stft = stft + initial Formula (subsequent): stft = stft + STEP

table.name: stft: idle time between step
table.description:
WITH IDLING ENGINE: Minimum time between stft corrections. At 1200rpm.
Formula: time = TIME_BETWEEN_STEP * 1200 / engine_speed

table.name: stft: lean rpm min
table.description: Minimum engine speed for lean correction. Below this speed and when a lean signal is detected while the stft is already enriching the stft is zeroed.

table.name: stft: limit
table.description: Maximum allowed negative or positive value for stft.

table.name: stft: o2 test runtime min
table.description: Minimum engine run time before the O2 test.

table.name: stft: o2 test threshold
table.description: Minimum O2 sensor voltage for the O2 test. Maximum O2 sensor voltage for the O2 test.

table.name: stft: runtime min
table.description: The engine has to be running at least this min amount of time, before the closed loop operation can be activated.

table.name: stft: smooth reactivity
table.description:
Every 5ms, the stft value is mixed with the previous value to build an average. The stft average is then used in the ltft.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: stft: stoich threshold
table.description: When the pre O2 sensor is above this voltage max, a rich condition is detected and the stft will enlean the mixture. When the pre O2 sensor is below this voltage min, a lean condition is detected and the stft will enrich the mixture.

table.name: stft: time between step
table.description: Minimum time between stft corrections.

table.name: stft: time use adj
table.description: If all corrections after this amount of time are insufficient, the step value is adjusted.

table.name: tc: abs ring teeth count
table.description: Number of teeth of holes of the abs ring. Needed to convert the abs signal into a rotational speed of the wheel.

table.name: tc: car speed reactivity
table.description:
At every cycle, the sampled car speed value is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: tc: engine speed min
table.description: When the engine is below this min speed threshold, disable the traction control.

table.name: tc: front speed threshold
table.description: When the car is above this max speed threshold, disable the traction control. When the car is below this min speed threshold, disable the traction control.

table.name: tc: front tyre circumference
table.description: Circumference of the front tyres. Needed to convert the rotational speed of the wheels into a car speed.

table.name: tc: fuelcut
table.description:
Every # stroke will have no fuel. The value 255 means no fuel cut.
Example: 8 means that 1 of 8 strokes will have no fuel.

table.name: tc: ign adv adj limit
table.description: Maximum ignition retard allowed for traction control.

table.name: tc: mode
table.description: Traction control mode.

table.name: tc: rear tyre circumference
table.description: Circumference of the rear tyres. Needed to convert the rotational speed of the wheels into a car speed.

table.name: tc: slip adj left right enable
table.description: Below this enable threshold for left-right wheel speed difference, the slip adj is immediately enabled (more slip allowed in straight line).

table.name: tc: slip min
table.description: When the slip is above this min value threshold, activate the traction control.

table.name: tc: slip reactivity
table.description:
At every cycle, the sampled slip value is mixed with the previous value.
Formula: value = (100% - REACTIVITY) * previous + REACTIVITY * sample

table.name: tc: slip target adj
table.description:
When the car is slipping the ecu apply a factor to the advance of the ignition. A retard to the ignition makes the engine loose torque.
Formula: ign_adv_adj = 100% - steps - (slip - base - adj) * slip_to_retard_ratio

table.name: tc: slip target adj time
table.description: Countdown duration before the slip target adjustment is disabled when a corner is detected.

table.name: tc: slip target base
table.description:
When the car is slipping the ecu apply a factor to the advance of the ignition. A retard to the ignition makes the engine loose torque.
Formula: ign_adv_adj = 100% - steps - (slip - BASE - adj) * slip_to_retard_ratio
Note: When using variable control knob: BASE = (knob_position / 2^BASE)

table.name: tc: slip to retard ratio
table.description:
When the car is slipping the ecu apply a factor to the advance of the ignition. A retard to the ignition makes the engine loose torque.
Formula: ign_adv_adj = 100% - steps - (slip - BASE - adj) * SLIP_TO_RETARD_RATIO

table.name: tc: time between step
table.description:
When the car is slipping, steps is increment at this time interval. And once the car is stabilised, steps is also decrement at this time interval.
Formula: ign_adv_adj = 100% - STEPS - (slip - base - adj) * slip_to_retard_ratio

table.name: tpms: pressure front
table.description: Tire target pressure sent to tpms module

table.name: tpms: pressure rear
table.description: Tire target pressure sent to tpms module

table.name: tpms: threshold front
table.description: Tire warning threshold sent to module

table.name: tpms: threshold rear
table.description: Tire warning threshold sent to module

table.name: tpms: use tpms
table.description: Tyre pressure management system.

table.name: tps: ctrl d gain
table.description: Throttle motor PID derivative gain (D = tps_dt x Kd / 10)

table.name: tps: ctrl i gain
table.description: Throttle motor PID integral gain (I = i_sum x Ki / 10000)

table.name: tps: ctrl i limit
table.description: Throttle motor PID integral term clamp magnitude

table.name: tps: ctrl p gain
table.description: Throttle motor PID proportional gain (P = tps_diff x Kp / 100)

table.name: tps: fallback
table.description: TPS value substituted when both TPS sensors are confirmed faulted.

table.name: tps: limit
table.description: Limit high target tps position (including the idle target tps position). Limit low target tps position (including the idle target tps position).

table.name: tps: motor duty cycle
table.description: Feedforward duty cycle to hold each throttle position against the return spring.

table.name: tps: period
table.description: Throttle motor PWM period

table.name: tps: smooth dec
table.description: Throttle responsiveness when releasing the gas pedal.

table.name: tps: smooth inc
table.description: Throttle responsiveness when pressing the gas pedal.

table.name: tps: smooth time between step
table.description: Throttle target smooth ramp timer period

table.name: tps: target
table.description: Target tps position for a given pps position.

table.name: vvl: coolant hysteresis
table.description: When the engine coolant is above this enable temperature threshold, enable the vvl. When the engine coolant is below this disable temperature threshold, disable the vvl.

table.name: vvl: duty cycle disengaged
table.description: Duty cycle of the pwm signal when the vvl is disengaged (low lift cam).

table.name: vvl: duty cycle engaged
table.description: Duty cycle of the pwm signal when the vvl is engaged (high lift cam).

table.name: vvl: high load hysteresis
table.description: HIGH LOAD: When the load is above this enable threshold, use the high load thresholds. HIGH LOAD: When the load is below this disable threshold, use the normal thresholds.

table.name: vvl: period
table.description: Period of the pwm signal for the vvl.

table.name: vvl: rpm high load hysteresis
table.description: HIGH LOAD: When the engine is above this enable speed threshold, engage the vvl. HIGH LOAD: When the engine is below this disable speed threshold, disengage the vvl.

table.name: vvl: rpm hysteresis
table.description: When the engine is above this enable speed threshold, engage the vvl. When the engine is below this disable speed threshold, disengage the vvl.

table.name: vvt: adv adj
table.description:
Intake camshaft advance adjustment.
Formula: adv = base - ADJ

table.name: vvt: adv high cam base
table.description:
Intake camshaft advance when vvl is engaged.
Formula: adv = BASE - adj

table.name: vvt: adv low cam base
table.description:
Intake camshaft advance when vvl is disengaged.
Formula: adv = BASE - adj

table.name: vvt: ctrl i gain
table.description: VVT cam angle controller integral gain

table.name: vvt: ctrl p gain
table.description: VVT cam angle controller proportional gain

table.name: vvt: period
table.description: Period of the pwm signal for the vvt.

table.name: vvt: rest pos threshold
table.description: VVT rest position deviation threshold; inhibits VVT and sets P0016

table.name: vvt: runtime min
table.description: The engine has to be running at least this min amount of time, before the vvt can be engaged.
